text: What if parenting is a survival strategy? Parental investment theory suggests that the effort parents put into raising offspring shapes species behavior. More investment means a greater chance of survival for the young. But it comes at a cost. In species like elephants, mothers invest years in their offspring, ensuring their success in a complex environment. But with uncertainty in resources, some species adopt a different strategy—having more offspring with less care. How do these strategies affect our understanding of evolution? Dive deeper into science with us!
